name: Avenc d'en Miquel
starting_point: Parking area at the top of the Costa d'en Blanes urbanization
equipment: 2 ropes of 30 m each, 1 bolt plate, several slings and protectors
description: A structural cave at the eastern end of the Pla de s'Aljub Gros with a main chamber of 16 x 21 x 3 m at a 35° slope. A shaft leads to an intermediate level and a larger lower level (4.5 x 18 x 12 m) of greater speleological interest. The cave is neither comfortable nor easy to navigate due to large unstable blocks and loose rocks, especially between the upper and lower levels.
full_description:
Description

At the eastern end of the Pla de s'Aljub Gros, this cavity of structural origin is found, formed by a main chamber of 16 x 21 x 3 m with an average slope of 35°. During the exploration and surveying work, a shaft was discovered in the eastern area, which has allowed the dimensions of the cavity to be extended, reaching a depth of 37 m. Ten meters from the start of the shaft, as a result of collapses that have occurred over time, there is a small level (Intermediate Level), and 16 m further down a larger one measuring 4.5 x 18 x 12 m, which we have called the Lower Level, of greater dimensions than the Intermediate and of greater speleological interest.

Although reconstructive processes are not very abundant, they have developed both on the Upper Level and especially on the Lower Level.

It should be noted that the Avenc d'en Miquel is neither a comfortable nor easy cavity to traverse. On the contrary, due to its current morphology in which large blocks and, perhaps worse, stones of all sizes threaten the visitor, especially in the section between the Upper Level and the Lower Level.

Rigging

A spit was installed at the top of the shaft, so that is the place where the required plate must be placed.

Note: The day's outing can be completed with a visit to the Cova Fosca, which is found in the surrounding area.
